raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Reviewing the section centrally includes assessing whether the contribution framing and thesis-level claims cohere with the described method.","R1.2":"The reviewer is expected to check whether the argument chain from method description to reported results to conclusion is internally consistent.","R1.3":"The section makes empirical claims about AUROC, escalation rate, and cost, so review centrally includes checking whether those claims align with the reported evidence.","R1.6":"A section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.","RX.1":"The projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of review is intended without inventing missing intent.","RX.2":"The review responsibility includes avoiding inflated interpretations of the method, results, or contribution claims.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities and not perform the review itself.","RX.4":"Because the artifact concerns medical imaging deployment, review responsibilities include attention to potentially harmful unsupported clinical implications.","RX.5":"The projected responsibilities should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact's explicit claims."}}

rationale.R1.1: Reviewing the section centrally includes assessing whether the contribution framing and thesis-level claims cohere with the described method.
rationale.R1.2: The reviewer is expected to check whether the argument chain from method description to reported results to conclusion is internally consistent.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes empirical claims about AUROC, escalation rate, and cost, so review centrally includes checking whether those claims align with the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.6: A section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of review is intended without inventing missing intent.
rationale.RX.2: The review responsibility includes avoiding inflated interpretations of the method, results, or contribution claims.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities and not perform the review itself.
rationale.RX.4: Because the artifact concerns medical imaging deployment, review responsibilities include attention to potentially harmful unsupported clinical implications.
rationale.RX.5: The projected responsibilities should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact's explicit claims.
